traps.description: No mutex: get/put mutate std::list and std::unordered_map without synchronisation.
traps.summary: get/put mutate the list and map with no mutex — a data race.

traps.description: get() promotes the entry it returns (splice -> list mutation), so it is NOT read-only. Using std::shared_mutex with a std::shared_lock on get() — the naive 'read-heavy' optimization — lets concurrent readers mutate the list and is a data race. Only an exclusive lock in get() is correct.
traps.summary: get() mutates the list, so a shared/read lock still races; needs an exclusive lock.

traps.description: Eviction loop uses > capacity instead of >= capacity; cache grows one entry beyond limit.
traps.summary: Eviction uses > instead of >= capacity, so the cache overgrows by one.

traps.description: capacity=0: eviction check (0 > 0) is false, so first put inserts an entry instead of being a no-op.
traps.summary: capacity=0 isn't a no-op; the first put still stores an entry.
